name: Pontevedra Peninsula - tour around O Grove and Sanxenxo
name_original: Península de Pontevedra - vuelta por O Grove y Sanxenxo
description: This difficult 36.8-mile (59.3 km) road cycling route around O Grove and Sanxenxo offers diverse coastal and rural scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling around Poio, situated on the northern bank of the Pontevedra estuary in Spain's Rías Baixas region, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area features a varied terrain, including coastal paths, riverine trails, and routes that traverse unique forests and hilly inland areas. Many routes consist of mostly paved surfaces, suitable for road cycling, with options ranging from gentle rides to paths with significant elevation changes. The region's geography provides a natural balcony overlooking the estuary, with an extensive coastline and numerous viewpoints. Best road cycling routes around Poio The most popular road cycling route is Pontevedra Peninsula - tour around O Grove and Sanxenxo, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain and coastal views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Playa de Baltar – View of Puerto de Sanxenxo loop from Poio, a moderate 36.9 miles (59.5 km) path that winds through coastal areas with views of the estuary. Local road cyclists also love the Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Soutomaior Castle loop from Pontevedra, a 38.5 miles (62.0 km) trail leading through historical sites and riverine trails, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Poio is defined by the Pontevedra estuary, varied coastline, and hilly inland terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle coastal rides to more challenging paths with significant elevation changes. The routes in Poio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Poio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Poio?
A: There are over 14 road cycling routes around Poio, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes with varied terrain, from gentle coastal paths to more challenging inland climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Poio?
A: Poio offers a varied terrain for road cyclists. You can expect coastal paths with stunning views of the Pontevedra estuary, tranquil riverine trails, and routes that traverse unique forests and hilly inland areas. Most routes feature mostly paved surfaces, ideal for road cycling.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Poio?
A: Yes, Poio offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 4 easy-rated road cycling routes that provide a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Poio?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Poio has 2 difficult-rated routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969123758/pontevedra-peninsula-tour-around-o-grove-and-sanxenxo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontevedra Peninsula - tour around O Grove and Sanxenxo&lt;/a&gt;, a 59.3 km trail with over 930 meters of elevation gain, offering significant climbs and rewarding coastal views.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover on road cycling routes in Poio?
A: Many routes in Poio offer splendid views. You can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Pontevedra estuary, Tambo Island, and Illote dos Ratos from coastal paths, such as those near Campelo dock to A Seca Recreational Area. The Boa Vista viewpoint on Monte da Tomba hillside also provides exceptional panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks I can visit while road cycling in Poio?
A: Yes, Poio is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can cycle through or near the beautifully preserved fishing village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Combarro&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique hórreos and traditional homes. The monumental &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Xoán de Poio&lt;/a&gt; is another significant site that can be incorporated into your routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Poio?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Poio are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Baltar – View of Puerto de Sanxenxo loop from Poio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31803529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Soutomaior Castle loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Poio?
A: Poio's mild climate makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes. Summer can be warm, especially on inland routes, but coastal rides benefit from sea breezes. Winter cycling is also possible, though some days may be cooler or wetter.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Poio?
A: The road cycling experience in Poio is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views of the Pontevedra estuary to the charming historical villages and varied terrain that caters to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs in Poio?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of or pass near local water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31802526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ría de Pontevedra – Lago de Castiñeiras loop from A Caeira&lt;/a&gt; includes views of the estuary and passes by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1532292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Castiñeiras&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/738182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontillón do Castro Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; on other routes.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate historical bridges or trails?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31803529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge – Soutomaior Castle loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt; specifically features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might find sections of routes that align with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/488410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tomeza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical context.

One of the most beautiful white sand beaches around Sanxenxo, Portonovo, about 650 meters long. It also has a marina and the fishing port is in operation. Good place to eat and drink after active cycling
NIce spot to take a break just before/after Sanxenxo. Great views.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "difficult" road ride, requiring "very good fitness." It covers approximately 59.3 kilometers with significant elevation gain, and some segments might be unpaved and challenging. It's best suited for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Rías Baixas region, including O Grove and Sanxenxo, enjoys abundant sunshine, making spring and early autumn ideal for cycling. The weather is generally pleasant, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, wetter winter months.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain, blending well-paved coastal roads with potential unpaved segments, especially on "Hard" routes. You'll cycle along continuous inlets, picturesque beaches, and through wooded areas, showcasing the natural beauty of the Rías Baixas region.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several stunning views. You'll find a View of Puerto de Sanxenxo and a View of Raxó and the Ría de Pontevedra. The Siradella Viewpoint, at 159 meters high, provides extensive panoramic views of the Ría de Arousa and the intertidal Umia-O Grove complex.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While the tour connects the towns of O Grove and Sanxenxo, you can typically find parking in either of these vibrant coastal towns to begin your ride. Both are popular tourist hubs with various parking options.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas in Galicia, bringing a dog on a demanding 59 km road ride is not typically recommended for their safety or comfort. If you plan to stop at beaches or establishments, check their specific pet policies.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. The trail passes through public roads and areas, including parts of the Complexo Intermareal Umia-O Grove, A Lanzada, Punta Carreirón e Lagoa Bodeira, which is a protected natural area but freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camiño Portugués Variante Espiritual and the Mtb Camiño Portugués Variante Espiritual. You might also encounter very short segments of local routes like R5 - Ruta da Chanca and R8 - Ruta de Bordóns.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route connects the popular tourist towns of O Grove and Sanxenxo, both of which are known for their vibrant atmosphere. You'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these towns and smaller villages along the way, perfect for a break or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Sunscreen is also highly recommended due to the abundant sunshine, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What kind of natural and ecological features can I observe?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the intertidal Umia-O Grove complex, which is an important ornithological reserve, offering unique opportunities for birdwatching. You'll also experience the diverse landscapes of the Rías Baixas, from open sea views to estuaries and wetlands.

waypoints.name: View of Raxó and the Ría de Pontevedra
